channel.title: The Grant Desk - Film & Video, added in last 30 days
channel.description: Paid open calls, fellowships and residencies in AI, tech, research, and digital and mixed-media arts, sorted into the right pile. Updated as new calls land on the desk. Filtered to: Film & Video, added in last 30 days.

item.title: Cork International Film Festival 2026: Call for Entries
item.description:
Organisation: Cork International Film Festival

Location: Cork, Ireland. Open to international entries; most strands require an Irish premiere.

Award: Screening at Ireland's oldest film festival (November 2026), including Academy Award and BAFTA qualifying shorts strands. FilmFreeway submission fee applies; fee waivers available by emailing info@corkfilmfest.org with the subject WAIVER REQUEST.

Duration: Festival runs November 2026. Features completed after 1 December 2025; shorts after 1 July 2025, max 40 minutes.

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

Cork International Film Festival, Ireland's oldest film festival, accepts fiction, documentary, animation, and experimental work across features and shorts, with Academy Award and BAFTA qualifying shorts strands. Late deadline for features, feature documentaries, international shorts, and documentary shorts is 31 July 2026 (Irish shorts have until 7 August 2026). Most strands require an Irish premiere. Fee waivers available on request.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Bloomberg Green Docs Competition 2026 (Short Climate Documentary)
item.description:
Organisation: Bloomberg Green

Location: Open call for filmmakers (see eligibility rules on the site); festival screening in New York City, USA.

Award: $25,000 grand prize awarded by a jury; the top five documentaries screen at the Bloomberg Green Docs Film Festival in New York City. No entry fee stated.

Duration: Winner announced at the festival on 22 September 2026

Deadline: 14 Aug 2026

The Bloomberg Green Docs competition is open to all eligible filmmakers competing for a $25,000 grand prize for a short climate documentary. The call seeks films that explore our climate future and reveal the world we are making today: ecosystems being restored, tomorrow's zero-carbon communities being formed, and habitat extremes warning of cascading climate consequences. REQUIREMENTS: films must address the urgency of climate change and run under 10 minutes including credits. The jury awards a $25,000 grand prize, announced at the Bloomberg Green Docs Film Festival in New York City on 22 September 2026, where the top five documentaries will be screened. Check the official rules on the competition site for eligibility details. DEADLINE: submissions accepted through 14 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Corridor: DAISY CHAIN Artist Commission (Adur, West Sussex)
item.description:
Organisation: Corridor Projects

Location: Adur and Worthing, West Sussex, UK. Open to a socially-engaged artist based in the South East of England working with moving image / lens-based media.

Award: GBP 4,200 artist fee (inclusive of VAT) paid in instalments, plus travel, and an additional budget for materials for creative production, workshops and exhibition outputs (e.g. print). No application fee.

Duration: September 2026 to July 2027: workshops September-January, post-production to February 2027, UK and Czech tour March-July 2027

Deadline: 03 Aug 2026

Corridor seeks a socially-engaged artist based in the South East of England working with moving image / lens-based media for DAISY CHAIN, marking the 60th anniversary of Vera Chytilova's experimental film Daisies (1966). The commissioned artist will work with a group of LGBTQIA+ young people aged 15-26 in Adur (West Sussex) to co-create new artworks: a 2-3 minute moving image work and a series of 2D collage or mixed media artworks for public exhibition and screenings. DAISY CHAIN commissions six new artist moving image works overall (three England-based artists working with older women, young women and LGBTQIA+ youth; three artists in Czechia), developed in partnership with the National Film Museum in Prague (NaFilM) and Brighton-based videoclub, with results toured across England and Czechia in 2027. EXPECTATIONS: devise about eight hands-on workshops incorporating film, collage and mixed media (participants won't necessarily have an arts background); participate in 4-6 online peer sessions on collaborative practice led by artist Annis Joslin; complete exhibition-ready artworks on time and to budget; maintain a transparent dialogue on authorship with participants. Artists must have a participatory practice and experience of working creatively with young people. Note: to create a safe space, workshops will not be publicly labelled as LGBTQIA+. FEE: GBP 4,200 incl. VAT plus travel and a materials budget. TIMELINE: shortlist notified 7 August, online interviews 12 August 2026; launch event in Adur/Worthing March 2027. DEADLINE: 10am, 3 August 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: RIFF: Reykjavik International Film Festival 2026 Submissions
item.description:
Organisation: Reykjavik International Film Festival (RIFF)

Location: Reykjavik, Iceland (festival 24 September to 4 October 2026). Open to international entries; minimum Iceland premiere.

Award: Screening at RIFF's 23rd edition. Submission fee 7,600 ISK (approx. EUR 60).

Duration: Festival 24 September to 4 October 2026. Films completed after 1 January 2025; shorts under 60 minutes and features, all genres.

Deadline: 24 Jul 2026

The Reykjavik International Film Festival accepts shorts (under 60 minutes) and features across all genres including documentary and hybrid work, completed after 1 January 2025, requiring at minimum an Iceland premiere. Late deadline 24 July 2026; submission fee 7,600 ISK (approx. EUR 60) via FilmFreeway.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: 41st Mar del Plata International Film Festival: Call for Entries
item.description:
Organisation: Festival Internacional de Cine de Mar del Plata / INCAA

Location: Mar del Plata, Argentina (festival 5-15 November 2026). Open to international entries; no prior public screening in Argentina.

Award: Selection in Latin America's only FIAPF A-accredited competitive festival (International and Latin American competitions, In Transit/WIP, Panorama). No submission fee stated on the registration page.

Duration: Festival 5-15 November 2026. Films completed November 2025 to November 2026.

Deadline: Closed 15 Jul 2026

The Mar del Plata International Film Festival is the only FIAPF A-list competitive festival in Latin America. The 41st edition accepts international entries across all genres for its International and Latin American competitions, the In Transit work-in-progress section, and Panorama. Films must be completed between November 2025 and November 2026 and not previously publicly screened in Argentina. Extended deadline 15 July 2026; submissions via the INCAA portal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mezipatra Queer Film Festival 2026 (27th edition) Submissions
item.description:
Organisation: Mezipatra

Location: Prague (5-12 November) and Brno (13-20 November), Czech Republic. Open internationally.

Award: Competition screening at Central Europe's main queer film festival. A small late-phase submission fee applies (free early bird ended 31 May).

Duration: Festival November 2026; selection notified by 12 October 2026.

Deadline: 20 Jul 2026

Mezipatra, Central Europe's leading queer film festival, screens in Prague and Brno each November. International queer-themed films can submit until 20 July 2026 (the free early-bird window ended 31 May; a small fee now applies). Selected films are notified by 12 October 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Field of Vision: Documentary Commissions (Rolling)
item.description:
Organisation: Field of Vision

Location: United States and international; filmmakers based anywhere.

Award: Commissioning fees and production funding (many films are commissioned assignments); specific amounts are not published publicly. No submission fee.

Duration: Varies by project (rolling)

Deadline: Rolling / undated

Field of Vision is a filmmaker-driven documentary unit that creates and commissions original short-form and feature nonfiction visual journalism. It funds and commissions filmmakers, often as assignments, and supports projects in development and production (it does not acquire finished films). SUPPORT: commissioning fees and production funding; specific amounts are not published publicly. ELIGIBILITY: filmmakers of all experience levels, within and outside the United States. APPLICATIONS are accepted on a rolling basis, with no fixed funding cycles or deadlines. Submit via the Field of Vision submissions page.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: LOOP City Screen / DOM Art Residence: Open Call for Video Artists (Barcelona)
item.description:
Organisation: LOOP Festival / DOM Art Residence

Location: Barcelona, Spain; work screened on public screens and facades across the city.

Award: Curatorial guidance, production support, and screening of selected video work across Barcelona public screens during LOOP Festival. Fee not stated.

Duration: Screenings during LOOP Festival Barcelona 2026.

Deadline: Closed 15 Jul 2026

Open call for international video artists to present work in urban space during LOOP Festival Barcelona, via the City Screen program with DOM Art Residence. Selected artists receive curatorial guidance, production support, and screenings across public screens and facades in Barcelona. DETAILS PARTIALLY UNVERIFIED: sourced from the Contemporary Lynx summer 2026 open-call roundup; confirm terms on the organizer's page before applying. Deadline 15 July 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Democracy at Stake in Europe: Short Documentary Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Guardian Documentaries and arte.tv

Location: Europe.

Award: GBP 10,000 in production support for one short documentary project, plus editorial and creative input from the partners and distribution through Guardian and arte.tv platforms.

Duration: Development and production of a single short documentary.

Deadline: 14 Sep 2026

Guardian Documentaries and arte.tv have opened a short documentary film fund on the theme 'Democracy at Stake in Europe', supporting the development and production of a character-led documentary short exploring threats, pressures and challenges facing democratic societies across Europe. The aim is to amplify personal stories that help audiences understand democracy in Europe today and to stimulate public debate; suggested areas of focus include press freedom, civic trust and democratic participation. AWARD: one project receives GBP 10,000 in production support; alongside the financial award, the selected filmmaker receives editorial and creative input from the partners, with distribution through Guardian and arte.tv platforms. The funding can be used for work linked to the film's development and production, such as research, filming and post-production, subject to the applicant's proposed budget and plan. ELIGIBILITY: open to European filmmakers aged 18 or over; the call is focused on a single short documentary and is intended for projects where individual experience and perspective are central to telling a wider story about democratic life in Europe. Deadline 14 September 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Mountainfilm: Commitment Grants & Emerging Filmmaker Fellowship
item.description:
Organisation: Mountainfilm

Location: Application online; Telluride, Colorado, United States (fellowship trip during the festival). Commitment Grants are open to filmmakers from any country; the Emerging Filmmaker Fellowship requires current U.S. residency.

Award: Over USD 90,000 in total funding. Individual Commitment Grants range from USD 3,000 to 6,000. The Emerging Filmmaker Fellowship adds a yearlong mentorship, a cash award, and a funded trip to Telluride over Memorial Day weekend (travel, lodging, and a festival pass).

Duration: Commitment Grants support projects in post-production; the Emerging Filmmaker Fellowship is a yearlong mentorship.

Deadline: 16 Jul 2026

Mountainfilm's Commitment Grants support documentary filmmakers with projects in post-production, with over USD 90,000 in funding this cycle for films that celebrate adventure, activism, social justice, culture, the environment, and indomitable spirit. Mountainfilm actively encourages diversity behind the camera, and a portion of funding is set aside for two focus areas: projects featuring an LGBTQ+ protagonist or a story about overcoming personal challenges, societal stigmas, and/or community obstacles with a meaningful connection to the outdoors or nature-based pursuits; and projects exploring immigration or migration, or created by immigrant or first-generation immigrant filmmakers. AWARDS: individual Commitment Grants range from USD 3,000 to 6,000. EMERGING FILMMAKER FELLOWSHIP: one early-career filmmaker is selected from the Commitment Grant applicant pool for a yearlong mentorship that includes edit and story consultation, networking opportunities, a cash award, and a funded trip to Telluride during the festival over Memorial Day weekend (travel, lodging, and a Mountainfilm pass, applicable to the year following the award). ELIGIBILITY: Commitment Grants are open to applicants from any country; the Emerging Filmmaker Fellowship accepts applicants from any country but requires current U.S. residency. Post-production projects only, completed, released, or festival-screened projects are not eligible, and impact campaigns are not funded. Prior Commitment Grant recipients should not re-apply for the same project; previously declined projects may re-apply only with substantial changes. REQUIREMENTS: a video sample or trailer of the current project no longer than 10 minutes (password-protected Vimeo links preferred); the screener link must stay active and cannot be changed after submission. The Emerging Filmmaker application additionally requires a director bio, headshot, and cover letter. Recipients are expected to provide progress updates 2-3 times a year, include Mountainfilm recognition in credits, and discuss premiere consideration with the programming team. TIMELINE: applications are open July 1 to July 16, 2026 (closing 11:59 PM MDT), with notifications in late August or early September. Questions: entries@mountainfilm.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Doc Society: Climate Story Fund
item.description:
Organisation: Doc Society (Climate Story Unit)

Location: Remote / international. Open to filmmakers of any nationality.

Award: Grants of up to USD 125,000. No application fee.

Duration: Supports late-production of non-fiction projects and the implementation of impact campaigns for completed and in-production projects.

Deadline: 24 Aug 2026

The Doc Society Climate Story Fund, run by Doc Society's Climate Story Unit, provides grants of up to USD 125,000 to support non-fiction projects in late production and the implementation of impact campaigns for both completed and in-production projects. The fund supports compelling independent media storytelling and impact strategies aligned with the Fund's mission around climate justice and biodiversity. This call has no set theme, welcoming applications across all areas aligned with the Fund's mission. Since 2021 the fund has distributed more than USD 4M to over 50 projects, with grantee work reaching distribution through outlets including BBC, POV, Netflix, Hulu, Disney+, Spotify, YouTube, and WhatsApp. WHAT YOU GET: up to USD 125,000 to support non-fiction projects in late production and impact campaigns. WHO IS ELIGIBLE: open to filmmakers of any nationality. DEADLINE: 24 August 2026. TO APPLY: see the fund page and apply via the Doc Society application. Questions: climate@docsociety.org.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Fondazione Prada Film Fund 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Fondazione Prada

Location: Milan, Italy-based fund; open worldwide to independent producers and production companies.

Award: EUR 1.5 million total pool supporting 10 to 12 feature films. Development grants EUR 15,000 to 80,000; production grants EUR 30,000 to 250,000; post-production grants EUR 40,000 to 100,000. No application fee.

Duration: Feature film funding (development, production, or post-production)

Deadline: 06 Aug 2026

The Fondazione Prada Film Fund is an annual EUR 1.5 million fund backing 10 to 12 feature-length films (fiction, documentary, and animation, 65 minutes or longer) across development, production, and post-production. ELIGIBILITY: independent producers and production companies with a track record (approximately two or more feature films premiered at major international festivals) and partial financing already secured; this is aimed at established independent producers, NOT individual emerging artists. DEADLINES: 6 August 2026 for projects in production; 10 September 2026 for development and post-production projects (this card uses the earliest deadline). Apply via the Fondazione Prada Film Fund page. NOTE: the funder's primary page was not directly reachable at time of entry; dates corroborated via industry trade press (Screen, Cineuropa). Verify the exact track deadline before applying.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Inside Out RE:Focus Fund Post-Production Grants 2026
item.description:
Organisation: Inside Out (Toronto 2SLGBTQ+ Film Festival)

Location: Toronto, Canada (Inside Out is a Canadian organisation; international projects may apply, see FAQ)

Award: Post-production grant funding to support the completion and promotion of short and feature films; the requested amount is set by the applicant (see the FAQ 'How much funding can I request?'). Since 2018 the RE:Focus Fund has distributed more than CAD 300,000 in grants and programs. No application fee.

Duration: One-off post-production grant

Deadline: 22 Jul 2026

Established in 2018 through a lead gift from Martha McCain, Inside Out's RE:Focus Fund addresses industry inequities by providing direct financial support to women, non-binary, and/or trans filmmakers telling 2SLGBTQ+ stories. Launched in 2019, the RE:Focus Fund Post-Production Grants support the post-production and promotion of short and feature films. Applications for the 2026 round open 29 June 2026. ELIGIBILITY: women, non-binary, and/or trans filmmakers whose projects tell 2SLGBTQ+ stories; per the FAQ, international projects may be eligible to apply. The requested funding amount is determined by the applicant, and unsuccessful applicants can request feedback and reapply the following year. Inside Out also runs other RE:Focus initiatives, including a new Residency program for first-time feature filmmakers facing intersectional barriers. KEY DATES: submissions open 29 June 2026, close 22 July 2026, with notifications by 15 August 2026. Apply and read the FAQ via the Eventival page linked from the RE:Focus Fund site; questions to industry@insideout.ca.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Basel House of Film Filmmakers Residency
item.description:
Organisation: Verein Filmhaus Basel und Umgebung (Basel House of Film)

Location: Basel, Switzerland

Award: Housing provided plus a stipend to cover travel and living costs; no fee to participate

Duration: 3 months

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The Basel House of Film, run by the Verein Filmhaus Basel und Umgebung, offers a three month residency for emerging artists working in film and television. Residents receive housing at the Filmhaus in the Basel old town plus a stipend to cover travel and living costs. There is no obligation to produce work, and residents can use the open schedule to write a script, prepare a film, draw storyboards, or edit. The facility includes a clubhouse, meeting room, office space, a small cinema with VR equipment, and an editing and grading suite. The program targets up and coming filmmakers, and both national and international applicants are welcome. Applications are accepted on a rolling basis through the online form, followed by a CV, motivation letter, and portfolio sent to the residency email.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: IDA Emergency Assistance
item.description:
Organisation: International Documentary Association

Location: Remote (worldwide applicants)

Award: One-time emergency grant of USD 500 to USD 5,000

Duration: One-time grant

Deadline: Rolling / undated

The International Documentary Association runs an Emergency Assistance program that provides direct financial support to documentary filmmakers whose work puts them in imminent and extreme physical danger. Eligible filmmakers may request a one-time emergency grant between USD 500 and USD 5,000. IDA responds to all applications within 24 hours and handles them with strict confidentiality, with approved funds transferred in as few as five business days. Applicants need an existing risk assessment plan, a concrete safety plan, two references confirming the emergency, and a secure way to receive funds. The program launched as a pilot on March 9, 2026 and accepts applications on a rolling basis. For sensitive cases, applicants can contact emergency@documentary.org to share information over an end-to-end encrypted app such as Signal.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: 100 Voices of Florida Film Grant
item.description:
Organisation: 100 Voices of Florida

Location: Florida, United States

Award: Up to USD 5,000 (unrestricted) USD 25 application fee with a Florida connection, USD 35 without

Duration: One-time grant

Deadline: 20 Sep 2026

100 Voices of Florida is a grant for filmmakers and screenwriters that awards up to USD 5,000 in unrestricted funds, which winners can use for finishing funds, festival submission fees, cast and crew reimbursements, production, or anything else. Applicants submit either a short film (under twenty minutes) or a script (twenty pages or less); films in other languages must include accurate English subtitles, and scripts must be in English. The grant is open to writers, directors, and producers who are at least 18 years old, and creative teams may submit, though only one entrant receives the award. A panel of judges with professional filmmaking experience evaluates entries, and the winner also receives a meeting with an industry professional. The deadline for the current cycle is September 20, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CASA PIP Residency for Filmmakers
item.description:
Organisation: CASA PIP

Location: Portugal; open internationally to filmmakers.

Award: Free residency (no cost to attend), offering time, space, and peace to work on a project or an early idea. A EUR 35 application fee applies.

Duration: Residency

Deadline: 31 Jul 2026

CASA PIP is a residency program in Portugal founded and run by filmmakers. It encourages residents to slow down and play, providing filmmakers with the time, peace, space, and general "rulelessness" to move meaningfully forward on their work, whether it is a whole project or just the budding idea for one. The residency is free to attend. ELIGIBILITY: open internationally to filmmakers. There is a EUR 35 application fee. Deadline: July 31, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CIRCLE Fiction Orbit 3rd Edition
item.description:
Organisation: CIRCLE Training Initiative

Location: Herceg Novi, Montenegro

Award: Accommodation and meals provided; in-depth script consultations, legal and production strategy guidance, career development mentorship, and psychological support. Travel not covered. Participation fee of EUR 2,000 per person applies. At least one 50 percent scholarship available annually for participants from lower-income countries, those without national funding, or with demonstrated financial need. EUR 2,000 participation fee (accommodation and meals included; travel not covered); 50 percent scholarship available for demonstrated need

Duration: One week

Deadline: Closed 10 Jul 2026

CIRCLE Fiction Orbit is a one-week retreat in Herceg Novi, Montenegro for up to six fiction feature film projects led by teams of women and gender-expansive filmmakers developing their second, third, or fourth narrative feature. Participants work with leading experts on script development, legal and production strategy, career positioning, and receive psychological support throughout the creative process. Applicants must identify as a woman or gender-expansive filmmaker, work as a screenwriter, director, or producer, have completed at least one feature, and submit a project with a producer attached. Accommodation and meals are provided, but travel is not covered, and a participation fee of EUR 2,000 per person applies. At least one 50 percent scholarship is available annually for those with demonstrated financial need. The third edition runs October 25 to November 1, 2026, with applications due July 10, 2026.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: CIRCLE Expanded Orbit 1st Edition
item.description:
Organisation: CIRCLE Training Initiative (with Onassis ONX)

Location: Athens, Greece

Award: No cash stipend. Provides individual mentorship from world-leading experts, group sessions, co-creation workshops, structured feedback, and access to an international professional network across film, art, design, and technology. Participation fee of EUR 2,000 per person (EUR 4,000 per team of two), with scholarships covering up to 50 percent available for those with financial need. EUR 2,000 participation fee per person (EUR 4,000 per team of two); up to 50 percent scholarships available

Duration: 6 days (in-person training)

Deadline: Closed 09 Jul 2026

CIRCLE Expanded Orbit is the first edition of an international training programme dedicated to immersive storytelling and XR-based practices, run by the CIRCLE Training Initiative in partnership with Onassis ONX. It supports women and gender-expansive filmmakers, artists, XR creators, and multidisciplinary storytellers working across XR, MR, VR, AR, AI, and game-based narratives, with no prior XR experience required. Selected individuals or teams of up to two receive individual mentorship, group sessions, co-creation workshops, and structured feedback, plus access to an international network of professionals. The in-person training takes place from November 21 to 26, 2026, in Athens, Greece. Applicants should be developing a project with a strong artistic vision and clear interest in immersive formats, ideally with a producer attached and partial budget secured. A participation fee of EUR 2,000 per person applies, with scholarships of up to 50 percent available based on financial need.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Genera Short Film Fund 2026 (current round)
item.description:
Organisation: Genera Films

Location: Global; open to short filmmakers worldwide (talent drawn from over 176 countries).

Award: Up to GBP 5,000 in funding for a short film project, any genre. Application requires either Pro Membership or a one-off funding fee (GBP 40 to GBP 50 depending on deadline tier).

Duration: Apply at any project stage: development, production, post-production, or festival circuit.

Deadline: Closed 30 Jun 2026

Genera Films offers up to GBP 5,000 in funding for a short film project, regardless of genre. Applications are welcome at any stage of a project's evolution: initial development, production, post-production, or preparing for the festival circuit. Applicants can either be Pro Members or pay a one-off funding fee that ranges from GBP 40 to GBP 50 (Earlybird GBP 40, Regular GBP 45, Final GBP 50); Pro Membership offers additional benefits described on the Membership tab. Established in 2017, Genera Films champions short filmmakers worldwide, drawing talent from over 176 countries and having supported over 50 projects as an independent patron. CURRENT ROUND TIMELINE: round opens 2 January; Earlybird deadline 28 February (GBP 40); Regular deadline 30 April (GBP 45); Final deadline 30 June (GBP 50); shortlist announced 10 July; funded films announced 17 July.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Future Vision XPRIZE (sci-fi film competition)
item.description:
Organisation: XPRIZE (Future Vision XPRIZE, funded by Peter Diamandis; partners Google and Range)

Location: Online / Worldwide (finals held in Los Angeles)

Award: USD 3.5 million-plus prize pool. Grand prize winner receives USD 2.6 million-plus to develop their vision into a feature film, with over USD 1 million in additional prizes for runners-up.

Duration: One-off competition; create a 3-minute sci-fi trailer

Deadline: 25 Sep 2026

Future Vision XPRIZE is a sci-fi film competition challenging filmmakers to portray an optimistic, abundant future for humanity rather than a dystopian one. CHALLENGE: create a 3-minute sci-fi trailer that depicts a bold, original, hopeful vision of progress. PRIZES: USD 3.5 million-plus total; the grand prize winner gets USD 2.6 million-plus to turn their trailer into a feature film, plus over USD 1 million in prizes for runners-up. BACKGROUND: launched by the nonprofit XPRIZE and funded by Peter Diamandis, with Google and Range as partners; inspired by the optimism of Star Trek and intended to give storytellers a hopeful 'north star' for builders. TIMELINE: competition launched 9 March 2026; finals on 25 September 2026 in Los Angeles (finalists attend in person). APPLY: register and read the rules on the contest website. NOTE: confirm the exact submission deadline, eligibility, and team rules on futurevisionxprize.com; the 25 September 2026 date listed here is the finals event date.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).

item.title: Small File Media Festival 2026 - Call for Work
item.description:
Organisation: Small File Media Society

Location: Canada; festival screening. Open to international submissions.

Award: No payment stated; festival programming and screening. No submission fee stated.

Duration: One-time festival screening.

Deadline: Closed 01 Jul 2026

The Small File Media Festival 2026 invites moving-image submissions under the theme "Can Small Files Save the World?" -- a call for arthouse, video work, glitch aesthetics, punk, sci-fi, narrative, and documentary work made with a small-file mindset. This year the festival especially welcomes small-scale experiments with narrative and documentary. The festival frames Small File Ecomedia as a political and ecological intervention: small files function in regions with precarious infrastructure, circumvent bandwidth imperialism and surveillance, and expose the environmental cost of mainstream online video. WHAT THEY WANT: craftily composed, elegantly performed, and artfully minimised movies in any genre; use of obsolete technologies, creative compression techniques, and low-bandwidth aesthetics at all stages of production is encouraged. Resources for making small-file work at www.smallfile.ca/make. Open to artists, activists, media pirates, rogue technologists, and filmmakers. No payment or submission fee stated. All submitted information used for festival programming and promotion -- ensure accurate spelling, punctuation, and capitalisation. Submissions close 1 July 2026 at midnight. Submit at https://smallfile.ca/submit/.

Open call details

Want to see more grants? Visit The Grant Desk (https://www.artificialnouveau.com/smalltools/grants/).
